pattern: Every triangulation of a polygon edge picks an apex vertex, splitting the span into two smaller polygons, which is the classic interval DP over vertex ranges.
hints: Fix the edge between the first and last vertex of a span; it must belong to exactly one triangle whose third corner is some vertex in between.
Choosing that apex breaks the span into a left polygon and a right polygon that you can solve independently.
Let dp[i][j] be the best score to triangulate vertices i..j; for each k between them combine dp[i][k] + dp[k][j] + values[i]*values[k]*values[j].

edge_inputs.description: hexagon mixing small and large weights
all equal values, choice does not change cost
alternating extremes reward isolating the big ones

stress.description: random polygons of varying size with a wide value range
stress.note: n stays small so the cubic DP and the oracle finish quickly
